component: scripts/k3s-discover.sh and durable k3s install identity handling
rootCause: A home power outage triggered DHCP reassignment for sugarkube3.local/sugarkube4.local/sugarkube5.local. Sugarkube internals persisted raw LAN IP identity in durable install/TLS SAN paths, so stale IP-based identity survived while actual interface addresses changed. Recovery was further complicated by a named-env parsing pitfall where `just up env=staging` could be interpreted literally (`env=staging`) in downstream discovery state.
resolution: Added a first-class Sugarkube outage record and changed k3s install/discovery defaults so durable TLS SAN identity is hostname-based (`<hostname>.local` + short hostname) without DHCP raw IP SAN by default. Kept raw-IP SAN and IP-first join URL behaviors as explicit opt-ins. Documented the `just up staging` workaround and stale Avahi cleanup used during incident recovery.
